CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prove-Up Hearing
Off Calendar Jan-03-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Maria Evangelista; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:08 am and concluding at 10:19 am.

Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau, Inc. appearing represented by Tiffanie Brown, Esq.
Defendant Exclusive Global Logistics, Inc. not appearing.

Plaintiffs counsel makes oral motion to waive the appearance of a witness and a motion shorting time on the motion to waive the appearance of a witness.

The Court Grants Plaintiff's oral motions.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

The Court reviews the Declaration of Benjamin Seeman.

The matter is submitted.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Creditors Adjustment Bureau, Inc., and against Defendant Exclusive Global Logistics, Inc., as follows:

1. Damages: $32,760.29
2. Prejudgment Interest: $ 16,010.36
3. Costs: $1,055.73
4. Attorney fees: $2825.00
5. Other: $4,572.95 (Sanctions ordered by the court on 7/6/23 and 9/5/23)

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $57,224.33

Proposed judgment signed and filed. (D505)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions, Striking Defendant'S Answer And Entering Default; Request For Monet Ary Sanctions Of $1,572.95; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Joseph Jyoo
Off Calendar Aug-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Jessica Sauber, Esq.213-223-2068Peitho Law APC for Defendant.
No other Appearance

RULING - A R G U E D. Counsel requests for a continuance. The Court informs Counsel the request needs to be addressed in the discovery calendar. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUREAU, INC.'S Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions. Transferred to be heard on the court's discovery calendar (room 301) on the same date at 9:00 a.m. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, STRIKING DEFENDANT'S ANSWER AND ENTERING DEFAULT; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $1,572.95; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT THEREOF; DECLARATION OF JOSEPH JYOO (MISCALENDARED IN LAW & MOTION)
Off Calendar Aug-22-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARNACES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. MICHAEL FRISCHER, Esq. of Law Offices of Kenneth J. Freed (Tel: 818-990-0888) for plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, STRIKING DEFENDANT'S ANSWER AND ENTERING DEFAULT; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $1,572.95.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for plaintiff (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's Motion GRANTED. No timely opposition filed. Plaintiff has submitted sufficient evidence to demonstrate that Defendant failed to comply with prior orders of the Court or otherwise respond to discovery. Thus, there is a history of failure to comply with discovery or the Court's orders. There is no evidence on this record that would suggest less severe sanctions would produce compliance with discovery. See Moofly Productions LLC v. Favila (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 1, 10-12 (collecting cases). Defendant's answer is STRICKEN. CCP 2023.030(d)(1). Plaintiff should submit Form Civ 100. The request for monetary sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $1,572.95, payable no later than August 31, 2023.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Plaintiffs First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions Of $1,572.95; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Joseph Jyoo
Off Calendar Jun-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances are made. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUREAU, INC. Motion To Compel Verified Responses To Plaintiffs First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Request For Monetary Sanctions Of $1,572.95

Moving party's second motion to compel responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One and for monetary sanctions is granted. No opposition filed. Responding Party shall serve code-compliant, verified responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One and pay to moving party, monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,000 within seven (7) days of service of notice of entry of this order.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem William Lynn at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: William Trupek; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
David Weeks, Esq. (818-419-2209; dweeks@kjfesq.com) of Law Offices of Kenneth J. Freed for Plaintiff.

Not argued. Discussion held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EXCLUSIVE LOGISTICS, INC.'S Motion To Transfer Venue.

Defendant's motion to transfer venue is denied.

Defendant signed an agreement with Plaintiff which specified "[i]f the Agreement to Arbitrate is found not to apply to your Claim, the parties consent and agree to both venue and personal jurisdiction in the federal and state courts located in San Francisco, CA." (Ex. 1, Terms of Service 13.6.3.)

Order is signed.
